title: Federal Way
name: Federal Way
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Federal Way's Highway 99 retail corridor, Commons at Federal Way, and light-rail multifamily near Seattle.

sections.html: Federal Way sits between Tacoma and Kent along the Highway 99 and I-5 corridor, and a buyer building a Federal Way identification package should expect a retail-heavy submittal centered on the Commons at Federal Way, with a growing multifamily component tied to the arriving Federal Way Link light rail extension. A qualified intermediary coordinating the closing should confirm construction status on the light rail extension before finalizing any identification tied to its arrival, since a delay there would affect the underwriting timeline more than the exchange mechanics themselves, and lender appetite for a station-area building can shift meaningfully once an opening date firms up.

sections.heading: The Commons at Federal Way and Highway 99 Retail
sections.html:
The Commons at Federal Way anchors the city's retail base as an enclosed regional mall, while the Highway 99 corridor running through the city carries a mix of older strip retail and auto-oriented commercial space that trades at a meaningfully lower basis than the mall itself. A buyer identifying retail here should be specific about which of the two categories a target property falls into, since comparable sales for an enclosed regional mall and a Highway 99 strip center do not overlap.

Vacancy along the Highway 99 corridor has been uneven in recent years, and a buyer should walk a target property's specific block rather than relying on a citywide vacancy average, since conditions can vary significantly within a few blocks. Ongoing redevelopment around the city's downtown core has also begun to reposition some older Highway 99 parcels, and a buyer should confirm whether a target property sits inside or outside that redevelopment boundary before assuming its current use will continue unchanged, since a parcel inside the boundary may face different zoning or permitting expectations at renewal.

sections.heading: Multifamily Ahead of the Light Rail Extension
sections.html: Federal Way's multifamily stock is building out in advance of the Link light rail extension reaching the city, and a buyer replacing into this submarket should confirm a target building's actual distance to a planned station rather than relying on a citywide description of Federal Way as light-rail-served, since the value premium concentrates tightly around the station itself. The statewide rent-cap law applies to this multifamily stock the same as anywhere else in the metro, and a buyer should model rent growth against the cap rather than assuming light rail alone will drive returns. Because Federal Way multifamily still trades at a lower basis than comparable product in Kent or Tacoma, it also functions as a landing spot for a buyer managing boot exposure on a downsizing exchange out of a higher-value relinquished property.

sections.heading: Light-Industrial Spillover From Kent and Auburn
sections.html: Federal Way's western edge picks up a modest amount of light-industrial and flex space spilling over from the larger Kent and Auburn corridors to its east, and this inventory trades at a discount to core Kent Valley product reflecting its smaller scale and slightly longer freeway access. A buyer should treat this spillover industrial as its own smaller category rather than assuming Kent Valley pricing applies directly to a Federal Way building.

sections.heading: Building the Federal Way Identification List
sections.html:
A workable Federal Way submittal typically draws from:

  • regional mall and enclosed retail at the Commons
  • Highway 99 corridor strip and auto-oriented retail
  • multifamily near the planned light rail station
  • light-industrial and flex space on the city's western edge

sections.heading: Sequencing Around the Light Rail Timeline
sections.html:
Because so much of Federal Way's multifamily upside depends on the light rail extension's completion date, a buyer should confirm current construction status directly rather than relying on an older published timeline, and should discuss that status with a lender before finalizing a multifamily identification here.

Buyers often pair a Federal Way multifamily identification with a Tacoma or Kent alternative on the same three-property list, so a construction delay on the light rail extension does not by itself jeopardize the exchange inside the 180-day period.

faqs.q: What is the difference between the Commons at Federal Way and the Highway 99 corridor?
faqs.a: The Commons is an enclosed regional mall trading on its own comparable set, while Highway 99 carries older strip and auto-oriented retail at a meaningfully lower basis, and the two should not be priced off each other or grouped into a single citywide retail average.

faqs.q: How does the light rail extension affect Federal Way multifamily value?
faqs.a: The premium concentrates tightly around the actual planned station, so a buyer should confirm a target building's real distance to the station rather than relying on a citywide light-rail-served description.

faqs.q: Is Federal Way multifamily subject to the statewide rent-cap law?
faqs.a: Yes, the same as anywhere else in the metro, and a buyer should model rent growth against the cap rather than assuming light rail alone will drive returns.

faqs.q: Does Kent Valley pricing apply to Federal Way's light-industrial space?
faqs.a: Not directly, Federal Way's spillover industrial trades at a discount to core Kent Valley product reflecting its smaller scale and longer freeway access, and a buyer should confirm which category a target building falls into before applying any Kent-derived cap rate.

faqs.q: Why pair a Federal Way identification with Tacoma or Kent?
faqs.a: A construction delay on the light rail extension could affect Federal Way multifamily timing, so pairing the identification with a nearby alternative keeps the exchange on track inside the 180-day period, and both neighboring cities offer comparable retail or industrial fallback options.
